title: <name> and the Great Family Road Trip Mix-Up

pages.text: “Everyone ready?” shouted Papa, honking the car horn for the fifth time that morning. Amma was still packing snacks, Dadi was stuffing wool shawls 'just in case,' and <name> was trying to fit her sketchbook, headphones, and lucky stuffed parrot into one tiny backpack.

Their plan: drive from Bengaluru to Ooty for a peaceful holiday. What could possibly go wrong? Everything. Ten minutes into the trip, Amma opened the snack bag and froze. '<name>! You packed your school bag instead of the food bag!' '<name> groaned. 'Oh no… I brought my homework to Ooty!'

Papa sighed. 'Well, we’ll eat your history notes if we get hungry.' Soon, the road twisted through forests and hills. '<name> leaned out the window, squealing, 'Look, elephants!' Papa squinted. 'Because, beta, Google Maps doesn’t have a setting for Elephant Zone!'

Dadi clucked. 'I told you, we should’ve taken the train!' They stopped at a roadside chai stall. The owner handed out steaming cups of tea and spicy pakoras. Kabir whispered, 'I think my tongue is on fire!' The chaiwala winked. 'That’s the flavor of adventure, beta!'

After another hour of driving, Papa proudly said, 'Look! We’ve reached the hotel!' Everyone tumbled out — only to realize the sign read: 'Welcome to the Maheshwari Wedding!' Before they could leave, a cheerful uncle draped garlands around their necks. 'The bride’s cousins, right?'

'Uh… yes?' <name> stammered, as Amma and Dadi were pulled into the dance. Soon, Papa was twirling under fairy lights while <name> was handed a plate of laddoos. 'This isn’t Ooty,' said Kabir, munching happily, 'but it’s fun!'

When the family finally escaped the wedding crowd and got back into the car, Papa said, 'Okay, this time we follow the map properly.' But the adventure wasn’t over. They stopped to take pictures at a 'beautiful lake' — which turned out to be a giant puddle. SPLASH! <name> slipped, soaking everyone.

Dadi laughed so hard her glasses fogged up. 'Now it really feels like a holiday!' After three wrong turns, one flat tire, and a monkey stealing Kabir’s chips, they finally reached Ooty — tired, sticky, and still giggling. The mountains shimmered in the sunset as cool air filled the car.

Amma sighed. 'I think we saw half of South India today.' <name> grinned. 'And danced at a stranger’s wedding!' Papa chuckled. 'Sometimes the wrong road takes you to the best memories.' That night, in their cozy hotel room, <name> opened her sketchbook and began to draw it all.

She drew elephants on the road, the dancing uncle, the stolen chips, and Dadi’s foggy glasses. On top, she wrote: 'The Great Family Road Trip Mix-Up — Best Trip Ever!' Papa whispered, 'Next time, we’ll use the train.' Dadi mumbled, 'Only if it stops for chai and pakoras!'
<name> smiled to herself. Even with wrong turns, lost snacks, and wild detours, one thing was certain — adventures are always better when they come with laughter (and maybe a wedding laddoo or two).
